content: MRSA diagnosis: 1) Culture on selective media (chromogenic agar) with cefoxitin disk diffusion or oxacillin screen; 2) Molecular detection of mecA/mecC genes by PCR (rapid, 1-8 hours); 3) Phenotypic confirmation with cefoxitin MIC. Blood cultures essential for suspected bacteremia (2 sets before antibiotics). Wound cultures from deep tissue/debridement preferred over superficial swabs. Nasal screening identifies colonization (up to 30% population colonized with MSSA, 1-2% with MRSA); decolonization with mupirocin nasal ointment BID x5 days + chlorhexidine body washes reduces surgical site infections. Vancomycin-intermediate S. aureus (VISA, MIC 4-8 mcg/mL) and vancomycin-resistant S. aureus (VRSA, MIC ≥16 mcg/mL) emerging; alternative agents required.
